title: ITP Reports 11 Compounds Including Astaxanthin and Alpha-Ketoglutarate Fail to Extend Mouse Lifespan
description: The NIA Interventions Testing Program evaluated 11 compounds in genetically heterogeneous mice and found none extended lifespan. Notably, astaxanthin, meclizine, and mitoglitazone, which previously showed promise, failed at different doses or starting ages. This underscores the difficulty of translating initial findings into robust anti-aging therapies.

title: FDA-Approved Drugs Terbinafine and Miglustat Extend C. elegans Lifespan via Mitochondrial Stress Response
description: Researchers discovered that terbinafine (an antifungal) and miglustat (a Gaucher disease drug) extend worm lifespan by activating the mitochondrial unfolded protein response (UPRmt). Because both are already FDA-approved, they could potentially enter human longevity trials faster than novel compounds.

title: On Aging 2026 Conference Opens Tomorrow in Atlanta With Thousands of Aging Professionals
description: The American Society on Aging's On Aging 2026, the nation's largest multidisciplinary aging conference, begins April 20 at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta. The theme is 'The Power of Belonging,' with sessions spanning public health, longevity research, policy, and community services through April 23.

title: Longevity Investment Tracker Shows $1.28B in Cellular Reprogramming, $835M in Age-Related Drug Funding
description: New Market Pitch's Longevity Segment Investability Tracker reveals that cellular reprogramming leads all segments with $1.28B raised across 6 deals over the past 24 months. Insilico Medicine alone accumulated $3.6B in pharma deal value. However, $4.7B in cumulative reprogramming funding has produced only one Phase 1 enrollment so far.

title: CNN's 'Kara Swisher Wants to Live Forever' Continues Weekly Run Exploring Longevity Science
description: The six-episode CNN Original Series following journalist Kara Swisher into the world of longevity science continues its Saturday 9pm ET run. The show has brought mainstream attention to longevity research, the 'don't die' movement, and the booming business of life extension.

title: ITP: 11 Compounds Including Astaxanthin, Alpha-Ketoglutarate, and Meclizine Fail to Extend Mouse Lifespan
description: The NIA Interventions Testing Program tested 11 compounds in UM-HET3 mice across three sites. None extended lifespan, and some were associated with reduced lifespan in females, highlighting the critical importance of dose and timing in aging interventions.

title: Terbinafine and Miglustat Extend C. elegans Lifespan Through UPRmt Activation
description: Two FDA-approved drugs, terbinafine and miglustat, were found to extend worm lifespan by activating the mitochondrial unfolded protein response. Their existing safety profiles could accelerate the path to human longevity trials.

title: Tau Seeds Propagate Through Brain via Neural Connectivity, Not Physical Proximity
description: New research shows tau protein seeds spread through the brain following neural connectivity patterns rather than simple physical proximity, offering insights into Alzheimer's progression and potential intervention targets.

title: 4 Healthy Aging Habits a Longevity Doctor Follows Most Days
description: A longevity-focused physician shares her daily anti-aging habits including strength training, sleep optimization with testing for sleep apnea, and intermittent fasting as core pillars of her routine.

title: On Aging 2026 Opens April 20-23 in Atlanta: The Nation's Largest Multidisciplinary Aging Conference
description: On Aging 2026, themed 'The Power of Belonging,' brings together thousands of professionals across public health, research, policy, and aging services at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta.

title: AGE Meeting 2026 in Utah to Cover Biomarkers, AI in Aging, and New Longevity Tech
description: The American Aging Association's 2026 annual meeting in Utah will feature sessions on aging biomarkers, AI applications in aging research, and emerging longevity technologies.

title: Longevity Segment Investability Tracker: Cellular Reprogramming Leads With $1.28B Raised
description: Two-year tracker shows cellular reprogramming at $1.28B (6 deals), age-related disease drugs at $835M (11 deals), and preventive health at $542M. Insilico Medicine alone accumulated $3.6B in pharma deal value.

title: PEARL Trial Update: Rapamycin Shifts From Theoretical to Experimental in Human Aging Research
description: A detailed analysis notes that 2026 marks the shift of rapamycin research from theoretical to experimental, with the PEARL trial showing safety at low intermittent doses and modest improvements in immune function and lean tissue mass.

title: USPTO Publishes AI-Driven Senolytic Compound Patent Covering 800,000+ Molecules
description: Patent application US20260097022A1 discloses screening of 2,352 compounds for senolytic activity and neural networks trained to predict activity across 800,000+ molecules targeting Bcl-2 proteins to reduce senescent cell burden.

title: CNN's 'Kara Swisher Wants to Live Forever' Explores Longevity Science and the Business of Life Extension
description: The six-episode CNN Original Series follows journalist Kara Swisher on a personal journey into longevity science, the booming business of life extension, and humanity's quest to cheat death.

title: $67B Longevity Boom and Big Tech Health Partnerships Highlight Week 16 Roundup
description: Longevity.Technology's Week 16 roundup covers the $67 billion longevity market boom, big tech health partnerships, and Peloton becoming the official movement partner for the Longevity Show 2026.

title: Life Biosciences' ER-100 Trial: Is the FDA Warming to Rejuvenation Therapies?
description: The FDA cleared Life Biosciences' IND filing for ER-100, an epigenetic treatment for partial cellular reprogramming in the human eye. This signals the FDA may be establishing a route for longevity-focused interventions.

title: Longevity Market Funding Roundup: Blueprint $60M, Function Health $298M, Aeovian $55M
description: Recent longevity funding includes Blueprint's $60M round backed by Kim Kardashian and Logan Paul, Function Health's $298M Series B, and Aeovian's $55M Series B for mTORC1 inhibitors targeting geroscience.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026, Up 56% Year-Over-Year
description: Q1 2026 saw 49 financing events totaling $3.74B, a 56% increase over Q1 2025. Full-year projections converge on $8-9B, implying 55-60% growth, as later-stage VC rounds and public market structures take larger shares.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026: Scientists Reframe Aging as Loss of Biological Coordination
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity in Berlin framed aging as a progressive loss of coordination between metabolism, immune system, mitochondria, and microbiota rather than a single fixable defect.

title: Longevity Biotech Reaches $3.74B in Q1 2026 Across 49 Financing Events
description: Capital flow analysis shows Q1 2026 longevity biotech investment at $3.74B across 49 events, targeting epigenetic reprogramming and mitochondrial health as key focus areas.

title: Dasatinib + Quercetin Senolytic Combo Triggers Unexpected Myelin Loss in Brain
description: A PNAS study from UConn's Crocker lab found that the popular senolytic combination D+Q triggers myelin loss in the brain, raising concerns about its prophylactic use while also offering insights into demyelination diseases like MS.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Labiotech profiles 11 companies at the forefront of anti-aging biotech including Altos Labs ($3B funding), highlighting partial epigenetic reprogramming, senolytics, and AI-driven drug discovery approaches.

title: ARPA-H Commits $144M Through PROSPR Program to Fund First Large-Scale Aging Clinical Trials
description: ARPA-H awarded $144M to seven teams to lay groundwork for Phase 3 trials of healthspan interventions. One team will test rapamycin, an SGLT-2 inhibitor, and semaglutide in healthy adults in their 60s.

title: Rapamycin Review: Safe at Low Doses for One Year But Insufficient Evidence It Slows Aging
description: Comprehensive analyses screening 500-948 papers conclude low-dose intermittent rapamycin appears safe over one year but lacks proof of slowing biological aging. The Kraig trial suggested a net 3.96-year biological age reduction but used daily dosing for only 8 weeks.

title: Brown University and Rochester Launch $22M ARPA-H Study to Slow Human Aging Process
description: A multi-institution project will test whether an HIV drug (Censavudine) can reduce DNA-triggered inflammation to slow aging. The trial will enroll 200 healthy adults aged 60-65 for 48 weeks across three clinical sites.

title: UTMB Launches Moody Longevity Trial Exploring Whether Medications Can Slow Biological Aging
description: The University of Texas Medical Branch launched the Moody Longevity Trial, funded by the Moody Endowment, to examine whether certain medications can slow biological aging in adults over 55.

title: 9 Anti-Aging and Longevity Startups to Watch in 2026
description: Profiles of promising longevity startups including YouthBio (FDA-supported Alzheimer's gene therapy), Life Biosciences (epigenetic reprogramming), and Junevity (siRNA for Type 2 diabetes), with investors noting 2026 as an inflection point.

title: David Sinclair Teases Exciting April 2026 Announcement With Lifespan.io
description: Longevity researcher David Sinclair posted 'Exciting! April 2026' referencing Lifespan.io, hinting at an upcoming announcement or collaboration in the longevity research space.

title: Clearing Senescent Cells Reduces T Cell Senescence and Inhibits Inflammaging
description: New research demonstrates that clearing senescent cells can reduce T cell senescence and inhibit inflammaging, supporting the therapeutic potential of senolytic interventions for immune system rejuvenation.
